outcome: Install a digest-pinned offline authority for the exact released OME-NGFF and Zarr artifacts used by the frozen profile.
Prove whether a pinned external reader can decode one hand-built array using the exact selected Zarr-v3 shard and codec subset.
invariants: The work stays outside Mirante production crates and remains off-product.
The producer does not import or invoke Mirante format, storage, identity, transform, statistics, or codec code.
External-reader success is diagnostic component evidence, not T1, IO-3, or writer-reader conformance.
The target-format fixture registry remains unavailable until the complete independent authorities are separately reviewed and promoted.
No fallback reader, alternate format path, or compatibility code may be added.
forbidden_paths: all product reader, writer, importer, runtime, renderer, and UI paths

authority.first_slice_predecessor_deletion: none: no machine-readable normative-artifact authority currently exists
authority.eventual_wp10a_c_deletion: the WP-06 false-only target-format guard is replaced atomically only when the complete reviewed target T1 authorities are promoted
unchanged: no CANDIDATE, IO-3, stable-format, or product-support claim

standards_scope.ome_ngff: 0.5.2 image metadata only, including the prose-only dimension_names requirement
standards_scope.claim: only the exact Mirante profile subset; never generic OME-Zarr or Zarr support

probe.input: one hand-built 2D uint8 root array using the frozen chunk, shard, key, zstd, CRC32C, and end-index configuration

non_goals: T1 archive, expected-fact, scientific-oracle, identity-vector, mutation, or promotion work
OME semantic readback or official-schema conformance claim
IO-3, full-package, PackageId, or ScientificContentId proof
format/profile/API changes, import, multiscale generation, replacement, or conversion
product wiring, product-open validation, performance qualification, or public-data release
stop_conditions: A normative artifact cannot be tied to an immutable official revision, license, exact bytes, and SHA-256.
The standards lock conflicts with the accepted profile freeze.
The probe requires any production-format or product change.
The external reader cannot decode the exact subset; record the failure and narrow interoperability instead of adding a fallback.
The work would promote T1, CANDIDATE, IO-3, stable-format, or product authority.
Any probe dependency enters the Rust workspace or product dependency closure.

acceptance: Every local standards artifact matches its source revision, byte length, and SHA-256 without network access.
Two clean probe runs yield the same metadata and shard digests and the external reader reports the exact expected geometry and values.
Both repository verification groups pass on the clean committed revision.
No product dependency, target-fixture promotion, or product authority changes.
evidence.product_open: not applicable because this slice changes no reachable viewer, loading, interaction, or rendering path

rollback.action: revert the checkpoint and delete ignored target probe output
rollback.product_rollback: none because no product authority flips
